name: Python
scope: An INTERMEDIATE Python developer, with 2-5 years experience, has a solid grasp of core programming concepts like data types, control flow, functions (including lambdas), and is adept with data structures such as lists, dictionaries, and sets. They are comfortable with object-oriented programming, able to create classes, implement inheritance, and understand polymorphism. They can manage modules and packages, use Python's standard library effectively, and handle third-party packages with tools like pip and virtual environments. Proficient in file operations and data serialization (JSON, CSV), they can interact with databases using SQL and ORMs like SQLAlchemy. They develop simple web applications using frameworks like Flask, consume and create APIs, and understand basic networking and concurrency (threading, multiprocessing). They write unit tests using unittest or pytest, debug code efficiently, and adhere to code quality standards like PEP 8 and best practices in documentation. Familiar with version control systems like Git, they collaborate using platforms like GitHub, participate in code reviews, and communicate effectively within a team. They can automate tasks with scripts, optimize code for performance, contribute to system design discussions, and improve existing systems while maintaining security awareness. Continuously learning, they stay updated with Python developments, seek feedback, and are capable of independent problem-solving on moderate-complexity projects.
long_scope:
An individual at the INTERMEDIATE proficiency level in Python is expected to have moved beyond the basics and can apply Python skills to solve real-world problems. They have a solid understanding of core concepts and can contribute meaningfully to projects. Below is a comprehensive scope of what an intermediate Python developer should be able to accomplish:

⸻

Core Programming Skills
	•	Data Types and Structures
	•	Proficient use of fundamental data types: integers, floats, strings, booleans.
	•	Advanced manipulation of data structures: lists, tuples, dictionaries, sets.
	•	Understanding of immutable vs mutable types.
	•	Control Flow
	•	Mastery of loops (for, while) and control statements (if, elif, else).
	•	Utilization of list comprehensions and generator expressions for efficient code.
	•	Functions
	•	Defining and invoking functions with positional, keyword, and default arguments.
	•	Understanding variable scope and lifetime.
	•	Use of anonymous functions (lambda).
	•	Modules and Packages
	•	Importing and organizing code using modules and packages.
	•	Familiarity with Python’s standard library modules (e.g., os, sys, datetime).
	•	Error and Exception Handling
	•	Using try, except, finally, and else blocks to handle exceptions.
	•	Raising exceptions and creating custom exception classes.

⸻

Object-Oriented Programming (OOP)
	•	Classes and Objects
	•	Creating classes with attributes and methods.
	•	Instantiating objects and accessing class members.
	•	Inheritance and Polymorphism
	•	Implementing single and multiple inheritance.
	•	Overriding methods and using super() to extend functionality.
	•	Understanding polymorphism and its application in Python.
	•	Encapsulation
	•	Using access modifiers to control access to class members.
	•	Understanding of getters and setters.

⸻

File and Data Handling
	•	File Operations
	•	Reading from and writing to text and binary files.
	•	Using context managers (with statement) for file operations.
	•	Data Serialization
	•	Working with JSON, CSV, and XML data formats.
	•	Serializing and deserializing data using json and pickle modules.
	•	Data Manipulation
	•	Using libraries like pandas and NumPy for data analysis.
	•	Performing data cleaning and transformation tasks.

⸻

Third-Party Libraries and Tools
	•	Package Management
	•	Installing and managing packages using pip.
	•	Understanding virtual environments (venv, virtualenv, conda) to manage dependencies.
	•	Popular Libraries
	•	Utilizing requests for HTTP operations.
	•	Implementing regular expressions with the re module.
	•	Basic plotting with matplotlib.

⸻

Web Development Basics
	•	Frameworks
	•	Building simple web applications using Flask.
	•	Understanding the basics of routing, templating, and handling HTTP requests.
	•	APIs
	•	Consuming RESTful APIs.
	•	Creating simple APIs to expose data or services.

⸻

Database Interaction
	•	SQL and NoSQL Databases
	•	Connecting to databases like SQLite or PostgreSQL.
	•	Performing CRUD operations using SQL queries.
	•	ORMs
	•	Using Object-Relational Mapping tools like SQLAlchemy to interact with databases.

⸻

Testing and Debugging
	•	Unit Testing
	•	Writing tests using unittest or pytest frameworks.
	•	Understanding test cases, test suites, and test runners.
	•	Debugging Techniques
	•	Using debugging tools or IDE features to step through code.
	•	Interpreting stack traces and logging output.

⸻

Version Control and Collaboration
	•	Git Proficiency
	•	Cloning repositories, committing changes, pushing, and pulling updates.
	•	Managing branches and understanding merge conflicts.
	•	Collaboration Tools
	•	Participating in code reviews using platforms like GitHub or GitLab.
	•	Documenting code changes and writing clear commit messages.

⸻

Code Quality and Best Practices
	•	PEP 8 Compliance
	•	Writing code that adheres to Python’s style guidelines for readability.
	•	Documentation
	•	Writing meaningful docstrings and inline comments.
	•	Generating documentation using tools like Sphinx.
	•	Refactoring
	•	Improving code structure and efficiency without altering external behavior.

⸻

Problem-Solving Skills
	•	Algorithmic Thinking
	•	Applying common algorithms and data structures to solve problems.
	•	Optimizing code for performance and scalability.
	•	Project Contributions
	•	Breaking down tasks into manageable components.
	•	Estimating time and resources required for development tasks.

⸻

Networking and Concurrency
	•	Basic Networking
	•	Understanding sockets and basic network communication.
	•	Working with higher-level network protocols.
	•	Concurrency
	•	Implementing threading and multiprocessing for concurrent execution.
	•	Understanding the Global Interpreter Lock (GIL) and its implications.

⸻

Automation and Scripting
	•	Task Automation
	•	Writing scripts to automate routine tasks (file management, data parsing).
	•	System Interaction
	•	Using Python to interact with the operating system and execute shell commands.

⸻

Security Awareness
	•	Secure Coding Practices
	•	Understanding common security vulnerabilities (e.g., injection attacks).
	•	Handling sensitive data securely (e.g., using environment variables).

⸻

Soft Skills and Teamwork
	•	Communication
	•	Effectively communicating technical concepts to team members.
	•	Continuous Learning
	•	Keeping up-to-date with the latest Python developments and libraries.
	•	Seeking feedback and actively improving coding skills.

⸻

Expected Tasks and Outcomes
	•	Develop Moderate-Complexity Applications
	•	Building applications that integrate multiple components (e.g., data processing pipeline).
	•	Maintain and Improve Existing Systems
	•	Debugging and fixing issues in existing codebases.
	•	Enhancing features and optimizing performance.
	•	Contribute to Design Discussions
	•	Participating in planning and architectural meetings.
	•	Providing input on technology choices and implementation strategies.

⸻

Tools and Technologies
	•	IDEs and Editors
	•	Proficient with tools like PyCharm, Visual Studio Code, or Sublime Text.
	•	Version Control Systems
	•	Comfortable using Git and platforms like GitHub.
	•	Databases
	•	Experience with relational databases (SQLite, PostgreSQL).
	•	Testing Frameworks
	•	Familiarity with unittest and pytest.
	•	Containers and Virtualization
	•	Basic understanding of Docker for containerization.

⸻

Mastery Over Concepts
	•	Efficient Coding
	•	Writing optimized and efficient code using appropriate data structures and algorithms.
	•	Modular Design
	•	Organizing code into reusable modules and packages.
	•	Error Handling
	•	Anticipating potential errors and writing robust code to handle exceptions gracefully.

⸻

Technical Expertise
	•	Independent Problem Solving
	•	Tackling assignments without constant supervision.
	•	Debugging Proficiency
	•	Quickly identifying and resolving code issues.
	•	Best Practices Application
	•	Implementing industry-standard practices in coding and development workflows.

⸻

Designing Systems and Processes
	•	System Design
	•	Contributing to the design of applications and systems.
	•	Process Improvement
	•	Suggesting and implementing improvements to development processes and workflows.

⸻

An intermediate Python developer is a valuable team member capable of handling a variety of programming tasks. They are not only able to write functional code but also contribute to the overall development process through effective communication, adherence to best practices, and a commitment to continuous learning.

name: Llamaindex
scope: A person with INTERMEDIATE proficiency level in Llamaindex as a competency should be able to design full Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipelines—data connectors → document/node creation → Vector, List, Summary, KeywordTable, KnowledgeGraph, SQL, or ComposableGraph indices → custom Retriever (k-NN, MMR, hybrid, metadata-filtered) → ResponseSynthesizer—while reasoning about hallucination, context window, latency and token cost. They ingest from S3, GCS, local FS, HTTP, GitHub, SQL, PDFs, HTML, Markdown or code bases using SimpleDirectoryReader or custom loaders, apply TextSplitter/NodeParser rules (token, sentence, heading, regex, semantic) and enrich nodes with metadata for ACL filters. They persist or load indices via StorageContext to local disk, SQLite, Pinecone, Weaviate, Chroma, Qdrant, Milvus, Redis or Elasticsearch vector plugins, perform incremental updates, shard/namespace large corpora and version indices for rollback. They swap embedding models (OpenAI, Azure, Cohere, HuggingFace/SentenceTransformers, Instructor-XL) and LLM back-ends (GPT-4, Claude, Gemini, llama.cpp local GGUF) through EmbeddingModel/LLMPredictor interfaces, engineer system/user/assistant prompts with templating, function-calling and response parsers, and chain them inside QueryEngine or external orchestration like LangChain, FastAPI, serverless functions. They incorporate conversational memory, multi-step query plans, citation formatting, streaming outputs and guardrails for output schemas, profanity or PII filtering. They benchmark similarity metrics (cosine, dot, Euclidean) and ANN parameters (IVF, HNSW, quantization), batch and async embed calls, cache in Redis/SQLite, monitor token usage, rate limits and implement exponential back-off. They build evaluation datasets, run RetrievalRecall, Faithfulness, AnswerRelevancy, precision/recall, F1, MRR or nDCG metrics, and conduct A/B tests across chunking, prompts and models. They secure secrets with Vault/AWS Secrets Manager/Azure Key Vault, encrypt vector stores, tag data for governance, and mitigate prompt-injection or jailbreak attacks. They automate recurrent ingestion with Airflow, Dagster, Temporal, Celery or cloud schedulers, containerize with Docker, deploy to serverless, microservice or Kubernetes clusters, configure autoscaling, liveness probes, blue-green or canary rollouts. They expose REST/gRPC or Slack/Teams bots, add callback handlers for Sentry, Datadog, OpenTelemetry, build dashboards in Prometheus/Grafana, Datadog or NewRelic. They document architecture diagrams, READMEs, Jupyter notebooks, maintain CI/CD (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, ArgoCD), review PRs, mentor juniors and keep change logs for prompts, embeddings and data connectors.
long_scope:
COMPETENCY SCOPE  
LlamaIndex – INTERMEDIATE (≈ 3–5 years hands-on)

Purpose  
Defines what an engineer at intermediate proficiency is expected to KNOW (conceptual grasp), be able to DO (typical tasks & deliverables), and USE (framework features, external tools, operational practices) when working with LlamaIndex to build retrieval-augmented, LLM-powered applications.

--------------------------------------------------------------------
1. Conceptual Mastery
--------------------------------------------------------------------
• Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) patterns  
  – Full understanding of the architecture layers: data connectors → index / storage → retriever → LLM → response synthesizer.  
  – Strengths, weaknesses and failure modes of RAG: hallucination, context loss, latency, token cost.  

• Embeddings & Vector Search Fundamentals  
  – Cosine vs. dot-product, Euclidean, inner-product similarity.  
  – Chunking strategies and how chunk granularity affects recall & precision.  
  – Role of metadata & filters in semantic search.  

• LlamaIndex Core Building Blocks  
  – Documents, Nodes, Metadata, Index Structs, Retriever, QueryEngine, ServiceContext, StorageContext, QueryConfig.  
  – Difference between built-in index types: Vector, List, Summary, Keyword Table, KnowledgeGraph, SQL, Composable Graph.  
  – Lifecycle of an index: build → persist → load → refresh / update → delete.  

• Prompt Engineering in LlamaIndex  
  – System, user, assistant roles; templating; parameter injection; response parsers.  
  – Prompt chaining inside LlamaIndex QueryEngine vs. external orchestration.  

• Observability & Cost Awareness  
  – Token accounting, rate limits, API usage throttling, exponential back-off strategies.

--------------------------------------------------------------------
2. Data Ingestion & Pre-processing
--------------------------------------------------------------------
What THE ENGINEER CAN DO  
• Connect to heterogeneous sources (S3, GCS, local FS, HTTP, GitHub, SQL databases, PDFs, HTML, Markdown, code bases).  
• Implement, configure, or lightly customize existing DocumentLoader/Reader classes; optimize them for throughput and resiliency.  
• Design and tune TextSplitter / NodeParser rules (by token, sentence, heading, regex, semantic) to balance context size and embedding quality.  
• Embed additional metadata (timestamps, authors, ACL tags, hierarchical section IDs) to enable fine-grained filtering during retrieval.  
• Automate recurrent ingestion with job schedulers (Airflow, Dagster, Temporal, Celery) or serverless cron (AWS EventBridge, GCP Cloud Scheduler).  

Outcomes  
– Clean, chunked, metadata-rich node sets stored in StorageContext (local, S3, DB).  
– Re-ingestion scripts idempotent and incremental, avoiding full re-builds.

--------------------------------------------------------------------
3. Index Construction & Management
--------------------------------------------------------------------
Tasks  
• Choose appropriate index type(s) based on data modality, query patterns and latency constraints.  
• Create hybrid or composite indices (e.g., Vector + KeywordTable) for more robust retrieval.  
• Persist indices with `persist_dir`, SQLite, or external vector DB adapters; verify compatibility and data integrity.  
• Execute partial updates: add/delete/merge nodes; rebuild embeddings selectively when chunking rules change.  
• Understand sharding, partitioning, and namespace usage when scale exceeds single-host memory.  
• Version indices with semantic naming or rev-hashes; roll back on regression.  

Tools / APIs  
– `VectorStoreIndex`, `ListIndex`, `ComposableGraph`, `StorageContext.from_defaults`, `SimpleDirectoryReader`, `download_loader`, `service_context = ServiceContext.from_defaults(...)`.  
– Pinecone, Weaviate, Chroma, Qdrant, Milvus, Redis, Elasticsearch vector plug-ins.

--------------------------------------------------------------------
4. Retrieval & Query Processing
--------------------------------------------------------------------
Capabilities  
• Configure custom Retriever objects (k-nearest, MMR, hybrid retriever, metadata-filtered retriever).  
• Apply structured filters (`metadata_filters`, `where_expression`) for scope-limited queries.  
• Handle multilingual queries with language-specific embedding models or translation middleware.  
• Modify or replace ResponseSynthesizer: refine prompts, streaming vs. non-streaming, citation formatting, highlighting.  
• Implement conversational memory with `ChatMemoryBuffer` or external data stores; manage token windows.  
• Orchestrate multi-step queries (top-k from each sub-index → aggregation → LLM synthesis).  

Outcomes  
– Accurate, context-rich, reproducible responses with traceable sources (citations).  
– Latency SLA ≤ few seconds for common workloads; graceful degradation/fallback when model or store unavailable.

--------------------------------------------------------------------
5. Integration & Extensibility
--------------------------------------------------------------------
• Swap out embedding models (OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, HuggingFace/SentenceTransformers, Cohere, Instructor-XL) through `EmbeddingModel` interface.  
• Integrate LLM providers (OpenAI Chat, Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini, Azure Functions, local GGUF via llama.cpp).  
• Compose LlamaIndex with LangChain, FastAPI, or custom microservices for orchestration.  
• Expose retrieval endpoint as REST/gRPC; wrap inside Slack/Teams/Bot front-ends.  
• Leverage tool & agent abstractions (OpenAI function calling, LlamaIndex Tool abstraction).  
• Add callback handlers for Sentry, Datadog, OpenTelemetry, custom logging.

--------------------------------------------------------------------
6. Performance Engineering
--------------------------------------------------------------------
• Optimize embedding batch sizes, async I/O, thread pools, rate limiting to respect OpenAI 90K TPM or vendor-specific quotas.  
• Implement local cache layers (Redis, SQLite, `sqlite_cache`, local directory).  
• Choose similarity metric and index parameters (IVF, HNSW, quantization) to minimize latency while preserving recall.  
• Benchmark with representative workloads; use built-in `LLMGraph` or custom scripts to measure latency, throughput, token cost.  
• Detect and mitigate memory bloat; offload large indices to disk-backed storage.  

--------------------------------------------------------------------
7. Evaluation & Quality Assurance
--------------------------------------------------------------------
• Build evaluation datasets (human labeled or synthetic via LLM-generated Q&A pairs).  
• Use `Evaluator` utilities: RetrievalRecallEvaluator, QueryResponseEvaluator, Faithfulness, AnswerRelevancy.  
• Compute precision, recall, F1, MRR, nDCG; monitor drift over time.  
• Apply guardrails (pydantic output schemas, profanity/PII filters, open source libraries like Guardrails.ai).  
• Conduct A/B tests across embedding models, chunking strategies, prompt revisions.

--------------------------------------------------------------------
8. Security, Privacy & Governance
--------------------------------------------------------------------
• Enforce data classification tags; design row-level or document-level ACL filters in retriever.  
• Store embeddings in encrypted datastores, secure secrets with Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault.  
• Implement prompt injection & jailbreak mitigations (input sanitation, output validation, safety prompts).  
• Abide by vendor policy on PII/PHI; anonymize or hash sensitive node text before embedding if required.  

--------------------------------------------------------------------
9. Deployment & Operations
--------------------------------------------------------------------
• Containerize pipelines (Docker, Podman); define CI/CD workflows (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, ArgoCD).  
• Deploy as serverless function, container app, or Kubernetes microservice; configure autoscaling rules based on QPS.  
• Provision indexes as separate microservices vs. monolith; use health checks, liveness, readiness probes.  
• Implement blue-green or canary rollout for index versions.  
• Configure monitoring dashboards (Prometheus/Grafana, Datadog, NewRelic) for latency, error rate, token usage, cost.  

--------------------------------------------------------------------
10. Collaboration & Documentation
--------------------------------------------------------------------
• Write clear README & architecture diagrams: data flows, index hierarchy, environment variables.  
• Adhere to code style, type annotations; add docstrings & Jupyter notebooks for reproducibility.  
• Train junior engineers on ingestion pipelines, basic retriever setup; review pull requests for prompt changes.  
• Track changes to prompts, embeddings, data connectors using version control & change logs.  

--------------------------------------------------------------------
What an INTERMEDIATE Professional Produces
--------------------------------------------------------------------
✓ Production-ready LlamaIndex pipelines feeding small-to-medium scale applications (tens of millions of tokens, up to hundreds of thousands of documents) with < 5 second p95 latency.  
✓ Automated, repeatable ingestion & indexing jobs, accompanied by monitoring and evaluation scripts.  
✓ Custom retrieval & synthesis behavior tuned for domain language, with clear citations and basic safety guardrails.  
✓ Integrations with at least one external vector store and one non-OpenAI embedding/LLM provider.  
✓ Documented architecture, deployment manifests, and operational playbooks.

--------------------------------------------------------------------
Boundaries of Intermediate Level
--------------------------------------------------------------------
NOT expected to invent new index structures from scratch, write core LlamaIndex PRs, or architect petabyte-scale distributed retrieval systems. Depth is sufficient to customize and optimize existing components, troubleshoot common issues, and make design trade-offs for moderately complex products; breadth covers mainstream integrations and emerging best practices.
